creator: jeffpc@josefsipek.net
text:
I freshly installed FreeBSD 14.0 and updated it to 14.0-RELEASE-p6.  After a `pkg install wsjtx`, wsjtx (2.5.4_10) starts up fine but the jt9 helper process (used to decode FT4 & FT8 among other modes) terminates because of a SIGBUS.  (That is, wsjtx runs fine collecting the rx audio, but at the end of the interval (e.g., 15s for FT8) it spawns jt9 to process it.)

I rebuilt the wsjtx port which didn't help.  However, using the unstripped binary led me to the following stack trace:

(lldb) bt
* thread #1, name = 'jt9', stop reason = signal SIGBUS
  * frame #0: 0x0000000820c4a2e8
    frame #1: 0x0000000000249e03 jt9`__ft8_decode_MOD_decode + 4931
    frame #2: 0x00000000002412e2 jt9`multimode_decoder_ + 4546
    frame #3: 0x0000000000238c4e jt9`jt9a_ + 814
    frame #4: 0x00000000002381ce jt9`MAIN__ + 7038
    frame #5: 0x000000000023890f jt9`main + 31
    frame #6: 0x000000082a852afa libc.so.7`__libc_start1 + 298
    frame #7: 0x00000000002365e0 jt9`_start at crt1_s.S:83

On a hunch, I started disabling security related sysctls which "fixed" the SIGBUS crash.  Specifically, I mucked with kern.elf64.aslr.enable. & kern.elf64.nxstack.

aslr=1 & nxstack=1 --> crash (this is the default on the system)
aslr=0 & nxstack=1 --> crash
aslr=0 & nxstack=0 --> wsjtx works as expected

I didn't try aslr=1 & nxstack=0 because I was too busy trying to use wsjtx to make contacts (to see if there were any other issues - I haven't found any).
